edges.description: TRPV4 channel activation raises chondrocyte calcium signaling and is linked to increased SOX9/SOX5/SOX6 activity, which can block orderly terminal growth-plate chondrocyte maturation.
edges.source: TRPV4 Channel Hyperactivity in Growth Plate Chondrocytes

edges.description: Blocked terminal chondrocyte maturation produces reduced hypertrophic chondrocyte organization and impaired endochondral replacement of cartilage by bone.
edges.target: Disrupted Endochondral Ossification and Chondrocyte Differentiation

edges.target: TRPV4 Channel Hyperactivity in Growth Plate Chondrocytes

nodes.description: Shortening of the digits accompanies the broader appendicular skeletal dysplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Radiographic findings show dumbbell-shaped long bones with widened metaphyses, very flat and dense vertebral bodies, a halberd-shaped pelvis, and brachydactyly with markedly delayed carpal ossification.

nodes.description: Markedly delayed carpal ossification is a characteristic hand radiographic abnormality.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Radiographic findings show dumbbell-shaped long bones with widened metaphyses, very flat and dense vertebral bodies, a halberd-shaped pelvis, and brachydactyly with markedly delayed carpal ossification.

nodes.description: Metatropic dysplasia causes disproportionate short stature with marked limb shortening; progressive spinal deformity later accentuates the altered body proportions.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Metatropic dysplasia is a clinical heterogeneous skeletal dysplasia characterized by short extremities, a short trunk with progressive kyphoscoliosis, and craniofacial abnormalities that include a prominent forehead, midface hypoplasia, and a squared-off jaw.

evidence.reference_title: TRPV4 related skeletal dysplasias: a phenotypic spectrum highlighted byclinical, radiographic, and molecular studies in 21 new families.
evidence.snippet: Autosomal dominant brachyolmia, spondylometaphyseal dysplasia Kozlowski type (SMDK) and metatropic dysplasia (MD) are currently considered three distinct skeletal dysplasias with some shared clinical features, including short stature, platyspondyly, and progressive scoliosis.

nodes.description: TRPV4 activation disturbs growth plate chondrocyte differentiation and endochondral ossification, producing severe vertebral and metaphyseal skeletal dysplasia.
nodes.id: Disrupted Endochondral Ossification and Chondrocyte Differentiation

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Histological studies in two cases of lethal metatropic dysplasia revealed markedly disrupted endochondral ossification, with reduced numbers of hypertrophic chondrocytes and presence of islands of cartilage within the zone of primary mineralization.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: These data suggest that altered chondrocyte differentiation in the growth plate leads to the clinical findings in metatropic dysplasia.

nodes.description: Long-bone morphology with dumbbell shaping is a characteristic radiographic finding.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Radiographic findings show dumbbell-shaped long bones with widened metaphyses, very flat and dense vertebral bodies, a halberd-shaped pelvis, and brachydactyly with markedly delayed carpal ossification.

nodes.description: Prominent forehead/frontal bossing contributes to the characteristic facial appearance.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Metatropic dysplasia is a clinical heterogeneous skeletal dysplasia characterized by short extremities, a short trunk with progressive kyphoscoliosis, and craniofacial abnormalities that include a prominent forehead, midface hypoplasia, and a squared-off jaw.

nodes.description: A halberd-shaped pelvis is part of the characteristic radiographic skeletal pattern.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Radiographic findings show dumbbell-shaped long bones with widened metaphyses, very flat and dense vertebral bodies, a halberd-shaped pelvis, and brachydactyly with markedly delayed carpal ossification.

nodes.description: Odontoid hypoplasia can contribute to cervical instability and warrants clinical surveillance.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Additional clinical findings, which may not be present in all cases, include odontoid hypoplasia with cervical instability and sensorineural hearing loss [Kannu et al., 2007; Geneviève et al., 2008].

nodes.description: Progressive joint contractures are part of the core musculoskeletal phenotype and contribute to worsening mobility.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: progressive joint contractures and distinct facial characteristics with a prominent forehead and squared-off jaw.

nodes.description: Progressive kyphoscoliosis is a major axial skeletal manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Metatropic dysplasia is a clinical heterogeneous skeletal dysplasia characterized by short extremities, a short trunk with progressive kyphoscoliosis, and craniofacial abnormalities that include a prominent forehead, midface hypoplasia, and a squared-off jaw.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal Dominant TRPV4-Related Disorders.
evidence.snippet: metatropic dysplasia. All TRPV4-related skeletal dysplasias are characterized by brachydactyly; the four most severe forms have short stature that varies from mild to severe with progressive spinal deformity and involvement of the long bones and pelvis.

nodes.description: Marked metaphyseal flaring/widening contributes to long-bone deformity.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Radiographic findings show dumbbell-shaped long bones with widened metaphyses, very flat and dense vertebral bodies, a halberd-shaped pelvis, and brachydactyly with markedly delayed carpal ossification.

nodes.description: Midface hypoplasia/retrusion is part of the characteristic facial gestalt.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Metatropic dysplasia is a clinical heterogeneous skeletal dysplasia characterized by short extremities, a short trunk with progressive kyphoscoliosis, and craniofacial abnormalities that include a prominent forehead, midface hypoplasia, and a squared-off jaw.

nodes.description: Respiratory compromise can begin in the neonatal period, particularly in patients with a small thorax.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Complications can include a small chest with some degree of respiratory compromise beginning in the neonatal period, and unrelenting kyphoscoliosis.

nodes.description: Vertebral flattening is part of the characteristic spinal radiographic pattern.

evidence.reference_title: TRPV4 related skeletal dysplasias: a phenotypic spectrum highlighted byclinical, radiographic, and molecular studies in 21 new families.
evidence.snippet: Autosomal dominant brachyolmia, spondylometaphyseal dysplasia Kozlowski type (SMDK) and metatropic dysplasia (MD) are currently considered three distinct skeletal dysplasias with some shared clinical features, including short stature, platyspondyly, and progressive scoliosis.

nodes.description: TRPV4 activation in chondrocytes is linked to increased SOX9/SOX5/SOX6 activity, which can restrain terminal growth-plate chondrocyte differentiation. The resulting loss of orderly prehypertrophic and hypertrophic chondrocyte columns bridges calcium-channel gain of function to disrupted ossification.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Activation of normal Trpv4 in the ATDC5 chondrocyte cell model was shown to increase the steady-state levels of Sox9 mRNA and protein and derepression of Sox6

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: It is possible that the activation of the TRPV4 channel in metatropic dysplasia leads to increased SOX9 and subsequently SOX5 and SOX6 activity, negatively regulating the terminal differentiation of growth plate chondrocytes.

nodes.description: Spinal canal narrowing or stenosis is a clinically important spinal complication reported in larger metatropic dysplasia care cohorts.

evidence.reference_title: Metatropic dysplasia-a skeletal dysplasia with challenging airway and other anesthetic concerns.
evidence.snippet: In addition to the airway difficulties, spinal canal narrowing or stenosis was widely prevalent, and no neuraxial anesthetic was performed in any of our patients.

nodes.description: Activating TRPV4 mutations increase basal calcium channel activity and likely raise intracellular calcium in chondrocytes, perturbing cartilage growth plate biology.
nodes.id: TRPV4 Channel Hyperactivity in Growth Plate Chondrocytes

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Electrophysiological studies demonstrated that the mutations activate the channel, indicating that the mechanism of disease may result from increased calcium in chondrocytes.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the calcium-permeable ion channel TRPV4 produce spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, Kozlowski type and metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Similar to autosomal-dominant brachyolmia, the mutations altered basal calcium channel activity in vitro.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Dominant mutations in the gene encoding TRPV4, a calcium permeable ion channel, were identified all 10 of a series of metatropic dysplasia cases, ranging in severity from mild to perinatal lethal.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the calcium-permeable ion channel TRPV4 produce spondylometaphyseal dysplasia, Kozlowski type and metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: The findings demonstrate that mutations in TRPV4 produce a phenotypic spectrum of skeletal dysplasias from the mild autosomal-dominant brachyolmia to SMDK to autosomal-dominant metatropic dysplasia, suggesting that these disorders should be grouped into a new bone dysplasia family.

nodes.description: Severe cases can have a small chest reflecting thoracic hypoplasia, which contributes to early respiratory compromise.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant TRPV4 mutations in nonlethal and lethal metatropic dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Complications can include a small chest with some degree of respiratory compromise beginning in the neonatal period, and unrelenting kyphoscoliosis.
